betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Fiberglass§f

§aHigh-performance insulation material — twice as effective as polymer insulation!§f

§bFiberglass§f is a synthetic silicate glass fiber polymer used for high-grade insulation. It's produced through the §bOre Acidizer§f using §bHydrogen Peroxide§f.

§6Production§f:
§b• Ore Acidizer§f: §e500mB§f Hydrogen Peroxide + §bSand§f → Fiberglass Bar
§b• Mining Laser§f: Sand with crystallizing upgrade also yields fiberglass

§6Uses§f:
§b• Insulation§f: Twice as effective as polymer (Teflon/Bakelite/rubber) — §e16§f vs §e8§f units. Same recipe as polymer (2 bars side by side)
§b• Soyuz Rocket§f: construction material
§b• FEnSU§f: power storage block
§b• Breeding Reactor Core§f
§b• Electric Arc Furnace§f: can be processed into silicon

§6Storage§f: Fiberglass bars compact into §bRolls of Fiberglass§f (block form) — useful as a durable construction material.

§eTip§f: Fiberglass insulation is twice as good as polymer-based options. Once you have Hydrogen Peroxide production, switch all your insulation recipes to fiberglass for maximum efficiency!
